title: Adjusting: Depreciation
event: Reyes Co. owns equipment that cost $48,000 with an 8-year useful life and no salvage value. Prepare the annual straight-line depreciation adjusting entry.

lines.label: cost ÷ life
lines.why: One year's slice of the equipment's cost.
lines.trap: Depreciation allocates COST over use — it does not track the market value of the machine.

lines.label: contra-asset builds
lines.why: A CONTRA-asset that piles up against the equipment.
lines.trap: Never credit Equipment itself. Original cost stays on the books; Accumulated Depreciation tracks the used-up portion.

memorize.body: Book value = Cost − Accumulated Depreciation. The balance sheet shows both, netted.

questions.prompt: Equipment cost $48,000, 8-year life, no salvage. Annual depreciation = ?

distractors.feedback: You expensed the whole machine in year one. Depreciation spreads the cost across all 8 years of use.
That's ONE MONTH (6,000 ÷ 12). The annual adjusting entry takes the full year: 6,000.

build.scaffold: Cost ÷ life for the amount. Debit the expense; credit the contra-asset — Equipment itself never moves.
